zh.All Saints Church, Stamford, Lincolnshir: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾斯坦福哥吉拉 沙也 邱奇
zh.Stamford, Lincolnshire, England: 英國林可爾史爾斯坦福
zh.Wragby Parish, Lincolnshire, England, Un: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當John Browne IV於1315年1月1日出生在英國林可爾史爾斯坦福時，他的父親Sir John Browne III36歲，母親Elizabeth Rebecca Yeidhall25歲。他於1338年3月8日與Alice Swinstead在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他於1376年被列為alderman of stamford (i.e. chief magistrate of the town, which had no mayor until the time of charles ii)。他去世於1398年9月8日在他的家鄉享年83歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾斯坦福哥吉拉 沙也 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Browne IV于1315年1月1日出生在英国林可爾史爾斯坦福时，他的父亲Sir John Browne III36岁，母亲Elizabeth Rebecca Yeidhall25岁。他于1338年3月8日与Alice Swinstead在英国英格兰林可爾史爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他于1376年被列为alderman of stamford (i.e. chief magistrate of the town, which had no mayor until the time of charles ii)。他去世于1398年9月8日在他的家乡享年83岁，埋葬在英国英格兰林可爾史爾斯坦福哥吉拉 沙也 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Browne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably also an Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Braune   or   Braun  .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Brown
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: generally a nickname referring to the color of the hair or complexion, Middle English br(o)un, from Old English brūn or Old French brun. This word is occasionally found in Old French, Middle English and Old Norse as a personal name or byname (Middle English personal name Brun, Broun, ancient Germanic Bruno, Old English Brūn, or possibly Old Norse Brúnn or Brúni). Brun- was also an ancient Germanic name-forming element. Some instances of Old English Brūn as a personal name may therefore be short forms of compound names such as Brūngar, Brūnwine, etc. As a Scottish and Irish name, it sometimes represents a translation of Gaelic Donn (see below). Brown (including in the senses below) is the fourth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below).
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Ó Duinn (see Dunn ) or for any of the many Irish and Scottish Gaelic names containing the element donn ‘brown-haired’ (also meaning ‘chieftain’), for example Donahue .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When John Browne IV was born on 1 January 1315, in Stamford, Lincolnshire, England, his father, Sir John Browne III, was 36 and his mother, Elizabeth Rebecca Yeidhall, was 25. He married Alice Swinstead on 8 March 1338, in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. In 1376, his occupation is listed as alderman of stamford (i.e. chief magistrate of the town, which had no mayor until the time of charles ii). He died on 8 September 1398, in his hometown, at the age of 83, and was buried in All Saints' Church, Stamford, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Browne IV est né le 1 janvier 1315, à Stamford, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Browne III, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Rebecca Yeidhall, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Alice Swinstead le 8 mars 1338, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. En 1376, sa profession de son indiquée est alderman of stamford (i.e. chief magistrate of the town, which had no mayor until the time of charles ii). Il est décédé le 8 septembre 1398, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 83 ans, et a été enterré à All Saints' Church, Stamford, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.All Saints Church, Stamford, Lincolnshir: All Saints' Church, Stamford, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als John Browne IV am 1. Januar 1315 in Stamford, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Browne III 36 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Rebecca Yeidhall 25. Er hat am 8. März 1338 in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom Alice Swinstead geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. 1376 ist sein Beruf als alderman of stamford (i.e. chief magistrate of the town, which had no mayor until the time of charles ii) aufgeführt. Er starb am 8. September 1398 im Alter von 83 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in All Saints' Church, Stamford, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Alice Swinstead il 8 marzo 1338, nel Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Nel 1376, la sua occupazione è indicata come alderman of stamford (i.e. chief magistrate of the town, which had no mayor until the time of charles ii). Morì il 8 settembre 1398, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 83 anni, e fu sepolto a All Saints' Church, Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: John Browne IVが1315年1月1日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアスタムフォードで生まれた時，父Sir John Browne IIIは36歳，母Elizabeth Rebecca Yeidhallは25歳であった。1338年3月8日にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアでAlice Swinsteadと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。1376年，職業はalderman of stamford (i.e. chief magistrate of the town, which had no mayor until the time of charles ii)と記録されている。彼は1398年9月8日に故郷で83歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアスタムフォードアル サイントス' チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.All Saints Church, Stamford, Lincolnshir: 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 스타퍼드 앨 세인츠' c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: John Browne IV(이)가 1315년 1월 1일 영국 링코실르 스타퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Browne III(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Rebecca Yeidhall(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1338년 3월 8일 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르에서 Alice Swinstead(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 1376년, 그의 직업은 alderman of stamford (i.e. chief magistrate of the town, which had no mayor until the time of charles ii)이었다. 그는 1398년 9월 8일 그의 고향에서 83세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 스타퍼드 앨 세인츠' c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Browne IV nasceu em 1 de janeiro de 1315, em Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Browne III, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Rebecca Yeidhall, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice Swinstead em 8 de março de 1338, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Em 1376, sua profissão aparece como alderman of stamford (i.e. chief magistrate of the town, which had no mayor until the time of charles ii). Ele faleceu em 8 de setembro de 1398, em sua cidade natal, com 83 anos, e foi sepultado em All Saints' Church, Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.All Saints Church, Stamford, Lincolnshir: алл сеинц' чурч, стамфорд, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Browne IV родился 1 января 1315 г. в местности стамфорд, линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Browne III было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Rebecca Yeidhall, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Swinstead 8 марта 1338 г. в местности линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. В 1376 году он работал по профессии alderman of stamford (i.e. chief magistrate of the town, which had no mayor until the time of charles ii). Он умер 8 сентября 1398 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 83 лет и был похоронен в местности алл сеинц' чурч, стамфорд, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Browne IV nació el 1 de enero de 1315, en Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Browne III, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Rebecca Yeidhall, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Alice Swinstead el 8 de marzo de 1338, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. En 1376, su ocupación registrada era alderman of stamford (i.e. chief magistrate of the town, which had no mayor until the time of charles ii). Murió el 8 de septiembre de 1398, en su pueblo, a la edad de 83 años, y fue sepultado en All Saints' Church, Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
